data.narrative:
SatRev is a Series B-stage space and aerospace company founded by Grzegorz Zwolinski. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 3 same-stage, same-sector private peers (Aalyria, Hubble Network, Sift) and a broader public comp set of 5 space and aerospace tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

The Peer-Median method is the primary applicable approach, anchoring on a median valuation of $400M across the 3 private comparables, with a range spanning $274M to $1.3B. The public-comp anchoring method—which leverages a median P/S multiple of 59.8x across the sector's 6 public peers—cannot be applied without disclosure of SatRev's revenue, a constraint typical for private companies at this stage.

The triangulated valuation range supports a midpoint of $400M, with a conservative bound near $340M and an upside scenario at $460M. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single quantitative method. Key uncertainties include SatRev's revenue run rate and growth trajectory relative to peer set, capital efficiency compared to Aalyria ($1.3B valuation) versus Sift ($274M), and stage-specific momentum in frontier aerospace fundraising. A Series B funding round would benefit from disclosure of core metrics—revenue, customer concentration, technical milestones—to inform a more granular valuation.
